description: A heap-buffer-overflow (out-of-bounds read) vulnerability exists in V8's String::Utf8Length function in src/objects/string-inl.h. The root cause is a type confusion triggered by corrupting the Map of an ExternalOneByteString to make V8 treat it as an ExternalTwoByteString. When a one-byte string is externalized via externalizeString(), a backing buffer of length N bytes is allocated. Using the V8 sandbox memory API (Sandbox.MemoryView with --sandbox-fuzzing), an attacker can overwrite the string's Map field with a two-byte external string map ID. Subsequently, when String::Utf8Length is called (e.g., via console.log), it invokes GetFlatContent which dispatches via DispatchToSpecificType using the corrupted Map, casting the object to ExternalTwoByteString and returning a FlatContent with a const uint16_t* pointer to the original N-byte buffer. The Utf8Length function then iterates over N uint16_t elements (requiring 2*N bytes), but the backing buffer is only N bytes, resulting in an OOB read of N bytes past the end of the allocation.

fixes.description: Upstream fix patch exported from commit 8c815e898fe13f9ed436825a77399561597cd708 for fixed-image builds.
fixes.evidence_text: Duplicate of issue 329781444; CL cages external strings in memory-corruption-api builds.
